reviews.review_msg: hard assignments, easy exams given you have a very very good understanding of his notes.

Bill is a great teacher for this course and actually has tried to teach programming, not just copying code samples.  Yes, the course isn't easy, but at least unlike every other course I've taken, he teaches it in a fun way with interesting assignments
Too Hard. An assignment was given about Constraints Satisfaction Problem (CSP) which is a upper division AI's problem.
hard and tough

For CMPT 201, it doesn't really matter what prof you take it with, as the entire course is designed to be completely standardized. Online lecture notes are good if you miss class, and be sure to keep up with assignments as they're worth more than half your grade.

He is a good teacher overall, but he gave many students (nearly half the class) a zero on one assignment after accusing them of cheating. Unfortunately, he did not give those students an opportunity to explain their side, and it feels as though he had already made up his mind.
Steve is a solid prof, but wasn't a fan of his super strict assignment policies. It's not hard to do well in his class though and I would still recommend him to most people.

Really good professor, lectures are worth attending, has good notes and you'll do great in his exams as long as you study properly. He does seem very harsh on his cheating policy, as I have a friend who did the assignment without AI or help and got 0 for a academic integrity violation.

Steve Ko is a very good professor. with extremely fair exams and a strict cheating policy. Although, I doubt every he caught did cheat as it was a lot of students; I would suggest to write your code as uniquely as possible to avoid his detection system. His github repo notes are great, as his in class notes are messy.

He gave 58 students (Almost half the class) 0 on a assignment accusing them of using AI since they have simillar patterns/functions in their code. It's also pointless to convince him otherwise, as he's quite confident in his thinking. As long as you don't write industry standard code, its a good class as exams are very fair.

Best professor! Genuinely care for students, and I love his way of teaching.
It really does make you go into classroom and pay attention to his lectures. Also, pls DO YOUR OWN WORK. Don't cheat

Pros: 
Cares a lot
Good lectures
Fair tests 

Cons: 
Didn't particularly like the assignments
Labs/in class assignments do take time but are worth very little 
Quiz marks affect assignment marks, so u ca mess up a quiz and it'll mess up assignment mark. It'd would have been better to separate the two and weight quizzes less.

Professor Ko is the best CS prof I've had so far. :) His teaching is super clear. The assignments are tough and take a lot of time, but they're totally worth it. Exams are fair. I'm sorry to hear a lot of students cheated on assignments. Please don't do that. You will definitely regret it when you get caught.

100/10 go to his lectures.  Lectures are compact but clear and organized; the textbook info is not hard to read but his lectures deliver the information better.  There are 5 long assignments (20-30hr) but they really help cement concepts taught in class.  I can't think of a better prof to teach 201 than Stevey :)

great prof hes rlly good at explaining concepts. there were some in class participation activities but overall easy to reach outside of class either in office hours or on piazza. super hard class but hes a great prof

Best professor I've had at SFU. His lecture style is incredibly straightforward and simple; never felt lost. Provided notes are well written and comprehensive. Assignments (later ones) are challenging but very rewarding. No surprises on the exams; what is taught is tested. Will remember 201 as a formative course in my education.

He makes a lot of sense in lectures and is very willing to help students understand. In class activies and labs were really helpful towards assignments. This class was assignment HEAVY. and they are extremely difficult. Plus the environment that we have to code on is really strict and not enjoyable. Great prof, hard course.

Initially disliked Steven for being strict, with no late submission policy. Homework is heavy but essential for CMPT201 and helpful. Exams are fair, covering only class topics. Lectures are excellent; he's passionate, with clear explanations and great notes. Tough class, great professor! Beware there are pop up class activities!

Steven is an excellent teacher! His lectures are engaging, and he explains concepts clearly, so don't miss class. The assignments are the hardest I've done in first 2 years but very rewarding—they teach you the concepts and improve your skills. Exams are fair, and if you complete the work yourself, you'll do well. Be ready to put in a lot of time!

He is very good at teaching!! The long assignments are heavy but that's just CMPT 201. Steve does a great job making sure everyone understands the concepts and he's fairly active on Piazza if you have questions about assignments. Definitely one of the better profs I've had so far.

Steve really cares about his students' learning. He provides lots of examples (code and conceptual) in lectures and is very engaging. The assignments are tough but definitely doable, exams are extremely fair. This course is quite dense (hence 4 credits) but it will completely level up your understanding of everything. So kind and patient, 11/10.

Good stuff, pretty chill guy and assignments are long but fair. also to whoever the yapper was in class that kept talking over him and all that PLEASE CONSIDER LEARNING TO RAISE YOUR HAND!!!!! and shower too buddy

Great professor, you can really tell he loves teaching. If the class didn't understand a concept, he would go over it again with a different example until mostly everybody gets it. Lectures were really engaging and Steve was very enthusiatic. The assignments were challenging, but they weren't impossible to figure out.
CMPT 201: Amazing prof. Very clear and gives candy during lectures. No surprises, your grade will be almost EXACTLY what you'll expect through autograders and very fair exams. Also hi Steve.

he gets the point across in his teaching but his lecture notes are disorganized
4/5 but if he gives me an A+ 5/5

Definitely don't miss lectures. The assignments were very time consuming but prof Ko was so good at teaching the course. I feel like my programming abilities went from a beginner to an intermediate because of all the practice and knowledge I gained from this course. The TAs were also very easy to talk to especially when asking for a regrade or help

The course is tough if you're coming straight from 125, but the workload is fair for 4 credits. Assignments align with lectures, and labs give a good amount of the code. Super nice guy who cares about the material and makes it easy to understand. If you put in the work and manage your time, it'll be easy to get a good grade. Would recommend 100%
he's just a chill guy
Never mess with this guy. The course was assignment-heavy. His teaching seems fine but assignments are just soo much tougher going beyond the lecture material.

Amazing professor. The lectures are simple and engaging. I liked that he used the white board.
The notes on Github are comprehensive and well written.
The later assignments are hard, yes, but they are incredibly well designed and DO drill the topics into your head. Was an amazing learning experience completing them.
Bro drops a  bomb just after/before every long assignment.

Honeslty, lecutres are good I cannot lie, but holly the assignments. They are the worst thing I have experienecd in this degree so far. They are a full time job that you cannot complete. They can take up to 40 hours easily then if you do not figure out one seg fault you just spent 40 hours in your week to get a 0 on a 10% assignemnt.

His long assignments are VERY difficult. You only get 2 weeks to work on each of them but some can take 30-40 hours or even more to complete. Besides that, his lectures and labs are alright. His tests are kinda annoying but aren't worth as much compared to the assignments altogether. Would not recommend 201 with him if you're fresh out of CMPT 125.

Steven Ko is extremely harsh and unforgiving when it comes to minor mistakes. I've never taken a class before where a misclick on an assignment could mean an F and possible academic dishonesty penalties. I just cant support a professor who is so dogmatic that he would punish even honest students for simple mistakes with his scorched earth policies.

The assignments are really difficult and time-consuming but he's a very good lecturer and thorough with his explanations. He knows what he's talking about and takes the time to answer any questions w/o making you feel dumb. He seems passionate about the topic but wanted to challenge students so the assignments are very hard :(. Exams were good tho!

CMPT201 in Spring 2024 is/was not a fun time. The large assignments are extremely difficult for a 2nd year course that can be taken by anyone fresh out of CMPT125. The grade distributions of the 3 large assignments (so far) have had an extreme disparity. Around ~40% of the class failed each assignment with grades almost like an inverse bell curve.
He give me lolipop
He's really accessible outside of class, lectures are really good. Overall Ko is very respected professor. Cmpt201 is worth taking, you'll learn a lot.

201 is a high workload and difficult course, homework can take 20+ hours and everything you do is recorded which is highly tedious if you make a mistake you may need to restart. Lectures are pretty dry and not worth going to, exams are pretty hard as well. Most of the material is just from Bryan Fraser.
He is caring and friendly, very approachable and ready to help you as much as he could

CMPT 201 is a hard course in general, so it's not entirely the professors fault for hard midterms/assignments (it is a 4 credit course). This is an assignment heavy course (66% of your grade) so beware of that. He also makes you take a quiz after an assignment is due based on the assignment which then determines your grade for the assignment.

tbh he grows on you, not over yet but I'd take it with linyi again i bet other profs make this course way worse and he doesn't

lectures are worth going to don't stop early on, most of the ppl who hate this course stopped going but if you stay it makes way more sense and linyi does his best to help you

This course sucks but I have no complaints really about Dr. Li, it's been decent with him and he clearly works very hard to make students understand. He is accessible and sincere and covers material at a reasonable pace, and is happy to answer questions. Course is insanely work-heavy (take fewer credits) but all versions of it are.
man his midterm is soooooooooooo hard
he's a good and very friendly guy, a little bit of stuttering but its fine.

I am in CMPT201 and while this is a freaking tiring hard course, it's clearly intended to be that way and not a reflection of Prof Li. He really cares about explaining in lecture, and does a lot of demos and reviews of quizzes/assignments.

Overall a really positive experience; this course is a beast though. But so far Dr Li has made it better.

AVOID THIS PROF AT ALL COSTS. DO NOT TAKE. Wait for Dr Brian or Steve to teach this class. This lord Stutters, unable to hear clearly, does not know what he is talking about. Be prepared to self study. Midterm was fair but final exam had a curveball with some random out of topic question requiring CMPT210 knowledge. Horrid prof.

Lecture attendance doesn't matter—fewer than 15 of 100+ students show up. The lectures just read the slides. Exams don't test understanding or coding, only memorization. Assignments take 10–20 hours and are worth 70%, but if you fail the exam average (15% each), they won't help. One bad exam can ruin your grade—drop early if caught off guard.
The glaze that Brian gets need to be studied. Ts is not that great.

Brian Fraser is AMAZING! His lectures are great and he spends some time going over actual code and giving coding exercises in-lecture, and they're even recorded, so you can watch them whenever! That said, the long assignments in the latter half of the course are VERY long, so do account for that when deciding your course load!

Great lecturer for a very intensive (particularly workload-wise) course. I learnt a lot, and while the workload (sections are standardized) was extreme at times (especially with the new quizzes/labs that I felt were disjointed at times i.e. what was expected to know), Brian's a very caring, experienced, and inspirational lecturer. Take it with him.

A LOT of assignment work but it's expected for a 4 credit course, midterm and final were very reasonable, quizzes weren't too bad if you did the assignments. What I really like about this prof is that he's very accessible outside of class, along with the TA's, and has lots of online office hours, which is very beneficial when u can't go in person

Brian is a great prof, with his lectures being informative and interesting.

His assignments are fun to do, though be aware of the long assignments. These take up to 30 hours to do. Even if you get 100% on one, you need a high grade on the quiz that unlocks your assignment's grade. The quizzes were obscure and shouldn't be underestimated.
Goat best prof at sfu

Makes difficult class seem not so bad. Offers lots of support through good, engaging lectures, and actively answering questions online and in person. The course does have a lot of assignments, be prepared to spend a lot of time on them, note each assignment has an accompanying quiz. Highly recommend to go to his lectures. Overall amazing prof.
NEVER TAKING HIM AGAIN

Best prof at SFU by every possible measure. Lectures are genuinely fun and useful. Assignments (while difficult) are incredibly rewarding to complete. Neovim sucks in the beginning, but it's not actually that hard (there's ~10 keybinds you genuinely need to memorize) and you get used to it quickly. Quizzes are easy if you just do the assignments.

The expectations in the course are wild! I mean you're somehow supposed to do the excessively long assignments, perform well/study for the difficult quizzes, remain caught up the heavy materials and lectures and also do the weekly labs. I mean this is easily the equivalent of 2 courses. Pair with easier classes or you'll probably suffer like i did
He's a good professor but makes very difficult quizzes for each assignment that can lead you together trying a much worse grade even after putting in all the time and effort.

Brian's one of the most respected and greatest profs in SFU's cs faculty. However, this sem he introduced the quizzes which cap your assignment grade. In order to get a full on assignment you need 5 or 6/6 on the quiz for that assignment. I don't think this does justice to the amount of time spent on assignments as quizzes are sometimes confusing

This was a tough course, but he included very helpful analogies and concise explanations. The exams were based on the assignments and lecture slides. He records lectures, but he brings a lot of enthusiasm and activities to his lectures so its worth going. The rumours about the long assignments are true so make sure to start those early.
One word: GOAT

Easily the best prof I've had at SFU so far. The course is dense, time-consuming and the process of learning neovim made me want to walk into the ocean and never come back. But in the end, it was worth it. The assignments are long but rewarding and now I have to stick to IDEs with neovim support otherwise it feels too slow. 10/10 would recommend.
Best CMPT prof, explains all concepts exceptionally well. All lectures are recorded so you are not required to attend. Always accessible for extra help. Provides detailed study guides for exams.

Lectures are engaging. Makes you want to go to class because you learn a lot and it isn't boring. Throws in humour, great analogies, and keeps a bright smile up. Don't think you can get a better computer science prof here at sfu.
Goated prof, quite possibly can't get any better than this for cmpt. long assignments require alot more time so be vigilant about that. Overall one of my best experiences in cmpt courses
straightforward, do the work, get the results.
